research_metadata.college_requested: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology
research_metadata.official_college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology
research_metadata.method_note: Official facts, institution-published claims, calculations, student reports and missing data are separately labelled.

college.full_official_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology
college.previous_name: Thapar University; originally Thapar College of Engineering and Technology/Thapar Institute history requires exact legal-name chronology confirmation
college.autonomous_status: Deemed-to-be university with academic and administrative autonomy
college.full_campus_address: P.O. Box 32, Bhadson Road, Patiala, Punjab 147004, India
college.campus_scope_note: All programme, cutoff, fee, hostel and qualitative records refer to Patiala Campus. Derabassi/LMTSM and COSE cutoff rows are excluded.

accreditation.value: Extension of Approval for 2025-26; Permanent ID 1-3688291341
accreditation.validity_period: Academic year 2025-26; 2026-27 EoA not publicly located

accreditation.value: Private deemed-to-be university with academic and administrative autonomy
accreditation.validity_period: Current; verify UGC listing at import
Exact programme validity dates require manual confirmation

accreditation.value: Civil, Mechanical and Mechatronics are described as NBA accredited on official department pages; exact current validity periods are not verified. Other programme-level statuses remain null.

admission_routes.admission_process: Institute application and online counselling based on JEE Main AIR for branches 1-9; JEE route also available for other eligible branches
admission_routes.eligibility: 10+2 in 2024/2025 or appearing 2026; Physics and Mathematics plus eligible third subject; 60% aggregate, 55% for SC/ST; must appear JEE Main 2026
admission_routes.category_reservation: As defined in the official admission brochure; 2% sports within Punjab seats and 2% Punjab Government nominees over and above intake
admission_routes.pwd_provisions: Minimum 40% permanent disability for PH category

admission_routes.entrance_examination: 10+2 PCM merit; JEE tie-break/requirements as officially notified
admission_routes.admission_process: For branches 10-16, admission may use JEE Main AIR or 10+2 PCM merit according to the official 2026 rule and vacancy/counselling schedule
admission_routes.eligibility: Official subject and percentage requirements from 2026 brochure

admission_routes.route_name: Biomedical Engineering and Biotechnology - JEE/NEET/PCB/PCM routes
admission_routes.entrance_examination: JEE Main AIR, NEET AIR, PCB marks or PCM marks
admission_routes.admission_process: Merit route depends on the applicant qualification and current official counselling schedule
admission_routes.spot_or_special_round: Direct admission may be offered subject to seat availability

fees.college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology

scholarships.college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology
scholarships.category_requirement: First 50 students irrespective of branch by JEE Main AIR
scholarships.benefit_description: 100% tuition fee plus development fee waiver
scholarships.duration: One academic year at a time; potentially renewable
scholarships.required_documents: JEE rank, 10+2 marks and institute scholarship documents
scholarships.renewal_conditions: Minimum 8.50 CGPA in preceding semesters, no backlog and all courses passed

scholarships.college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology
scholarships.category_requirement: Next 60 students irrespective of branch by JEE Main AIR
scholarships.duration: One academic year at a time; potentially renewable
scholarships.required_documents: JEE rank, 10+2 marks and institute scholarship documents
scholarships.renewal_conditions: Minimum 8.50 CGPA in preceding semesters, no backlog and all courses passed

scholarships.college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology
scholarships.category_requirement: First 10% of admitted students in each discipline, excluding over-and-above intake
scholarships.entrance_exam_requirement: JEE Main 2026 AIR; PCB rules apply separately to BME/BT
scholarships.duration: One academic year at a time; potentially renewable
scholarships.renewal_conditions: AGPA at least 8.00, no backlog, all courses passed and within top 10% of on-roll discipline

scholarships.college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology
scholarships.applicable_programme: BTech Biotechnology or Biomedical Engineering - PCB admission
scholarships.category_requirement: Top PCB-based institute rank across eligible branches
scholarships.entrance_exam_requirement: Institute rank based on PCB marks

official_verified.headline_capacity_claim: Over 8,500 boys and about 4,000 girls; requires plausibility/manual confirmation
official_verified.allotment_policy: Subject to availability; room/hostel changes restricted; students residing within 50 km are not eligible under the stated rule
official_verified.mess: Mandatory; no opt-out; charges tentative and consumption-linked
official_verified.accessibility: NIRF reports ramps/lifts and accessible toilets in more than 80% of buildings, with walking aids/transport support
official_verified.official_curfew: Main gate by 8:00 pm and hostel by 8:30 pm under the published hostel rules; nightly attendance
official_verified.vehicle_rule: Students may use bicycles; powered vehicles are not allowed under published hostel rules

student_reported_notes.summary: Hostel social life is commonly valued, but room, washroom, mess and Wi-Fi experiences vary by block.

location.college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology
location.official_address: P.O. Box 32, Bhadson Road, Patiala, Punjab 147004, India
location.nearest_airport: Shaheed Bhagat Singh International Airport, Chandigarh
location.nearest_major_hospital: TIET Health Centre (on-campus); Government Rajindra Hospital for major external care
location.technology_ecosystem: On-campus research/innovation and entrepreneurship ecosystem; Chandigarh-Mohali technology ecosystem is roughly 65-75 km away
location.cost_of_living_band: Moderate relative to major Indian metros; highly lifestyle- and housing-dependent
location.data_origin: Address/airport/rail official; coordinates and local travel distances map-derived; cost band inferred from student-town context

senior_junior_culture.summary: Public AMAs and society discussions show accessible peer guidance and cross-batch interaction, especially through Frosh activities, hostels and technical societies. Mentorship quality appears network- and society-dependent. The reviewed evidence is insufficient for a college-wide statement about hierarchy or ragging incidence.
positive_themes: Students and placed seniors answer applicant and junior questions publicly.
Societies, hostels and project teams create cross-batch contact.
negative_themes: Mentorship is not uniform and may depend on the student network.
No robust recent independent evidence on club hierarchy or fresher inclusion across the whole campus.

academic_culture.summary: Student reports describe a workload that is manageable for organized students but varies by branch and instructor. CGPA is repeatedly described as important for scholarships, internships and placement shortlisting. Faculty accessibility and attendance strictness receive mixed reports, so no single institute-wide strictness label is justified.
positive_themes: Official curriculum and societies support project, coding and research activity.
Students report room to combine academics with societies when time is managed well.
negative_themes: Faculty and grading experience appears instructor-dependent.
High CGPA pressure is a repeated student concern.
academic_culture.source_agreement: Consistent on CGPA importance; mixed on strictness and teaching quality

hostel_reality.summary: Official documents show a large residential system with multiple occupancy and AC/non-AC options, mandatory mess, free laundry and a strict 8:00/8:30 pm first-year gate/hostel reporting structure. Videos and student reports confirm substantial block-to-block variation in rooms, common areas, Wi-Fi and social experience.
positive_themes: Residential life and peer bonding are commonly praised.
Multiple room categories and on-campus services are available.
Free laundry and mandatory dining infrastructure are officially documented.
negative_themes: Room and washroom condition vary by block.
Wi-Fi reliability and mess satisfaction are inconsistent across reports.
Room type is subject to availability and official curfew may feel restrictive to some students.
hostel_reality.source_agreement: Consistent on variation and social benefits; mixed on quality

club_and_campus_life.summary: The 2026 institutional catalogue lists a broad technical, cultural, service and entrepreneurial ecosystem, and student discussions consistently identify societies and fests as major parts of campus life. Individual society activity and selection quality vary; a static listing is not proof that every society is currently active.
positive_themes: Wide range of technical and cultural choices.
Fests and society work provide project, leadership and social opportunities.
First-year activity is supported through Frosh and society recruitment.
negative_themes: Students report that some societies are more active than others.
Selection processes and time commitment can be uneven.

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official recruiter and placement-cell pages, computing societies and student accounts indicate an active preparation ecosystem. The strongest student-reported cautions are CGPA screening, market-cycle pressure, large computing intake and branch-dependent access to software roles. Verified outcomes are available only at overall NIRF level; branch-level online statistics remain unverified.
positive_themes: Peer guidance, internships and self-driven coding routes are visible.
A broad official recruiter roster exists.
negative_themes: Company-wise branch eligibility is not publicly transparent.
Community branch placement tables are not official.
Core-branch software access and 2026 outcomes are disputed.
coding_and_placement_culture.source_agreement: Consistent on preparation culture; conflicting on branch outcomes and ROI

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is in Patiala, a lower-cost and quieter city than a major technology metro. Railway and local city access are practical; Chandigarh airport is around 65 km away. Students often describe the campus itself as the main social ecosystem, while major-city internship networking requires travel or remote engagement.
positive_themes: Large self-contained campus with daily services.
Patiala railway and local autos/e-rickshaws provide practical connectivity.
Lower day-to-day cost than many major metros is plausible.
negative_themes: Airport and Chandigarh/Mohali technology ecosystem require road travel.
Students seeking constant metropolitan exposure may find the location quieter.

overall_satisfaction.summary: The most repeated positives are the campus, hostels, societies, peer network and broad opportunity set. The most repeated negatives are high fees, uncertain branch-level ROI, large intake and market-sensitive placements. Satisfaction appears especially dependent on scholarship support, chosen branch, CGPA and willingness to use societies/projects rather than relying only on classroom teaching.
positive_themes: Strong opportunity set for self-driven students.
negative_themes: Outcomes depend strongly on branch, CGPA and individual preparation.
who_it_appears_best_suited_for: Students seeking a residential campus and active society culture.
Students who can afford the full cost or have a realistic scholarship plan.
Self-driven students prepared to build skills and internships early.
who_may_not_enjoy_it: Students prioritizing the lowest-cost engineering option.
Students expecting guaranteed software placement from any branch.
Students who strongly prefer a major-metro environment or dislike strict hostel timings.
overall_satisfaction.would_choose_again_evidence: Public opinions are mixed and too self-selected for a reliable percentage.
overall_satisfaction.source_agreement: Positive campus themes repeat; ROI and placement judgments conflict

parent_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based only on collected evidence
parent_mode_summary.total_cost: Published four-year academic charges including admission range from INR 20.188 lakh to INR 26.30 lakh. Current-rate academic plus hostel/mess calculations span roughly INR 25.108 lakh to INR 34.732 lakh; these are calculated estimates, not guaranteed totals.
parent_mode_summary.scholarship_possibilities: Strong merit and merit-cum-means schemes exist, including full tuition/development waivers and partial waivers. Renewal is conditional on high CGPA/AGPA and no backlogs.
parent_mode_summary.placement_stability: Verified NIRF medians were 10.0, 11.89 and 10.0 LPA for 2021-22 to 2023-24; branch-specific stability cannot be verified.
parent_mode_summary.travel_and_medical: Patiala railway access is practical; Chandigarh airport is about 65 km/around 1.5 hours. TIET has an on-campus health centre and 24/7 ambulance.
parent_mode_summary.hostel: Large residential system with mandatory mess, varied room types and officially published strict timings; exact room/rate depends on availability.
parent_mode_summary.academic_discipline: CGPA strongly affects scholarship and student-reported placement screening. Attendance/faculty strictness varies by branch and evidence is mixed.

student_mode_summary.label: Perspective summary based only on collected evidence
student_mode_summary.branch_fit: Branch choice matters substantially; do not assume uniform software-role eligibility. Historical AIML and current AI & DS should not be treated as identical until confirmed.
student_mode_summary.coding_and_core_culture: Computing, open-source, cybersecurity, robotics and domain societies provide peer-learning routes, but outcomes depend on individual participation and CGPA.
student_mode_summary.clubs_and_campus_life: Residential campus, societies and fests are repeated strengths; individual clubs vary in activity and selection quality.
student_mode_summary.academic_flexibility: Projects and societies are accessible, but time management, attendance and high CGPA requirements can constrain flexibility.
student_mode_summary.hostel_experience: Hostel life supports peer bonding, while rooms, mess and Wi-Fi vary by block; official timings are restrictive for some preferences.
student_mode_summary.internships_and_placements: Build skills and internships early; use official overall data cautiously and demand branch-specific evidence before comparing ROI.
student_mode_summary.city_exposure: Patiala is quieter and less metro-like; Chandigarh/Mohali exposure requires travel.
student_mode_summary.placement_roadmap: Year 1 fundamentals and society exploration; Year 2 DSA/domain projects and CGPA; Year 3 internships and branch-specific preparation; Year 4 targeted applications and interviews.

decision_risks.evidence: Published academic charges including admission are about INR 20.188 lakh to INR 26.30 lakh, before hostel/mess and personal costs. Current-rate four-year academic plus hostel/mess estimates span roughly INR 25.108 lakh to INR 34.732 lakh.
decision_risks.mitigation: Budget without assuming scholarship renewal and confirm annual hostel/fee revisions.

decision_risks.evidence: Official structured cutoffs were found for 2024 and 2025 plus 2026 Round 1; no official 2023 table was located and 2026 is not final.
decision_risks.mitigation: Do not present the current file as a complete three-year final series; obtain archived 2023 and later 2026 rounds.

decision_risks.evidence: The 2026 programme table totals 3,390 seats, materially above earlier NIRF approved intake.
decision_risks.mitigation: Verify class organization, branch pooling and final seat matrix; assess placement capacity using branch-level denominators.

decision_risks.evidence: NIRF verifies overall outcomes but current branch-wise eligible, registered and placed counts are not officially public in the reviewed sources.
decision_risks.mitigation: Request an official branch-wise CILP report and avoid relying on anonymous compilations.

decision_risks.evidence: Official recruiter pages do not state branch eligibility, while student reports conflict on non-CSE software access.
decision_risks.mitigation: Choose the branch on curriculum fit and verify company eligibility history separately.

decision_risks.evidence: Major scholarships require 7.5-8.5 CGPA/AGPA, no backlogs and annual reselection.
decision_risks.mitigation: Model affordability at full fee and treat scholarships as conditional.

decision_risks.evidence: Room categories differ substantially, mess is mandatory, rates can change and block quality is student-reported as uneven.
decision_risks.mitigation: Budget for a higher-cost room fallback and verify the allotted block/rate before payment.

decision_risks.evidence: Official hostel rules specify main-gate entry by 8 pm and hostel entry by 8:30 pm with attendance.
decision_risks.mitigation: Confirm whether current rules differ by year/gender/permission process and assess lifestyle fit.

decision_risks.evidence: Patiala offers a large self-contained campus but major technology-market access requires travel toward Chandigarh/Mohali or remote networking.
decision_risks.mitigation: Use societies, alumni, remote internships and planned travel for industry exposure.

data_gaps: Final 2026 cutoffs after all counselling, direct-admission and vacancy rounds conclude.
2026-27 AICTE Extension of Approval and a final counselling-code-to-programme mapping.
Exact category-wise, sports, nominee, NRI, supernumerary and PH seat counts for each branch.
Current original NBA accreditation letters and validity dates for every UG programme.
Branch-wise placement records with eligible, registered, placed, average/highest CTC, PPO and internship counts.
Dated recruiter visits, roles, eligible branches, selection counts and packages.
Confirmed 2026-27 Patiala hostel/mess rates because the fee PDF contains a stale-session note.
Actual boys/girls hostel bed capacity by block.
Current activity date and recruitment process for every listed club.
Independent multi-source evidence on faculty accessibility, grading strictness, ragging incidence and student repeat-choice satisfaction.

conflicts.source_2_value: NIRF reported approved UG intake of 2,500 for 2023-24
conflicts.possible_reason: Different academic years, new programmes and major computing-intake expansion

Confirm scholarship application windows and required documents.

Confirm hostel bed capacity, first-year allotment, curfew and vehicle rules.
